message:
fix: Improve error handling in Rust presynaptic kernel

Replaces unwrap() calls with proper error propagation for missing
dictionary keys in the presynaptic state.

Changes:
- .get_item("KEY")?.unwrap() → .get_item("KEY")?.ok_or_else(|| PyValueError)

Improvements:
- Provides clear error messages when state dictionary is missing required keys
- Uses PyO3 exception types for proper Python-side error reporting
- Prevents panic!() crashes with informative ValueError exceptions
- Explicitly names missing keys in error messages for easier debugging

Affected Keys:
- C (calcium concentration)
- BUF (buffer occupancy)
- RRP (readily releasable pool)
- RES (reserve pool)
- PR (primed fraction)
- CL (clamp strength)
- E (energy/ATP)

This change improves the robustness of the Rust/Python interface and
provides better diagnostics when the presynaptic state dictionary is
malformed or incomplete.

🧠 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
